source_issue.title: title_or_id wakes up the real object

summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue identifies a performance concern in Plone. When using the `title_or_id` method on catalog results (known as "brains"), it triggers a retrieval of the real underlying object, which is not efficient.

## What needs to be done
The goal is to optimize the `title_or_id` functionality in `plone.app.contentlisting.catalog.CatalogContentListingObject` to avoid unnecessary object retrieval. Suggested approaches include modifying the current implementation to return title and ID directly from the brain, rather than calling `getObject`.

## Helpful background
The `title_or_id` method is currently not included in the catalog metadata, which leads to the performance issue. There are multiple discussions on potentially adding this method directly to the `CatalogContentListingObject` class, but consensus on the exact implementation is still unclear.

## Good first steps
1. Review the `CatalogContentListingObject` in the Plone codebase, focusing on the relevant section: [catalog.py](https://github.com/plone/plone.app.contentlisting/blob/master/plone/app/contentlisting/catalog.py).
2. Experiment with the proposed code modifications noted in the comments, particularly by adding `title_or_id` directly and ensuring it retrieves data efficiently.
3. Test any changes thoroughly, and consider documenting your approach in a follow-up comment.
4. Coordinate with other contributors in the issue discussion for review and feedback on your work.
